syntax_check.evidence: Completed with exit code 0; test file is syntactically valid.

red_test.summary: 5 tests failed, 0 passed. Failures are assertion failures against missing v2 control-plane registry behavior, not syntax errors.

failures.test: project add creates a v2 registry/projects.json under LAUNCHDECK_HOME
failures.evidence: Expected created=true and v2 registry payload; current CLI returned success without the new created/project alias fields.

failures.test: projects reads legacy LAUNCHDECK_HOME/projects.json when v2 registry is absent and migrates on mutation
failures.evidence: Expected migrated ProjectRegistration alias/projectId fields; current legacy registry read returns old shape without alias.

failures.test: project add keeps projectId stable across alias repair and rejects alias collisions
failures.evidence: Expected project repair to succeed; current CLI exits 1 because repair/alias behavior is not implemented.

failures.test: project add refuses to mutate an unsupported newer registry version with repair guidance
failures.evidence: Expected state_version_unsupported refusal; current CLI exits 0 and ignores the unsupported v2 registry path.

failures.test: project repair updates project path config and alias metadata without changing identity
failures.evidence: Expected repair success with before/after/changes; current CLI exits 1 because project repair is not implemented.

recovery_guidance: Implement the v2 control-plane registry writer at LAUNCHDECK_HOME/registry/projects.json with ProjectRegistration.projectId, alias, status, timestamps, and created/updated result flags.
Keep read compatibility for legacy LAUNCHDECK_HOME/projects.json when the v2 registry is absent, then write only the v2 shape on mutation with migratedFrom metadata.
Add alias parsing and uniqueness checks under registry mutation, returning alias_conflict with existingProjectId details.
Fail closed with state_version_unsupported for newer registry versions and preserve the incompatible state file unchanged.
Add project repair to update alias/projectRoot/configPath metadata without changing projectId.
concerns: The focused tests intentionally fail until production state-store and CLI registry behavior are implemented by later lanes.
